codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully integrates pthread_kill() into the signal delivery mechanism to enable actual inter-process signal delivery. The change adds a single line calling pthread_kill(task->thread, SIGUSR1) in __iox_apply_signal_to_task(), which ensures target threads receive POSIX signal notifications when signals are sent via iox_kill() or iox_killpg(). This completes the signal infrastructure by bridging the simulated signal state (pending signal sets) with actual thread notification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the AGENTS.md naming conventions (iox_ prefix for functions, IOX_ prefix for macros). The implementation respects the signal delivery pattern documented in AGENTS.md which mentions using pthread_kill with SIGUSR1 as a signal trigger.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: signal.c:144-148 - pthread_kill(task->thread, SIGUSR1) follows the documented pattern in AGENTS.md 'Signal Delivery Pattern' section

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation contract requirements VAL-PROCESS-020 through VAL-PROCESS-025 were already fully satisfied by the existing signal implementation. The pthread_kill addition enhances the implementation by ensuring actual signal delivery to target threads, but the contract requirements were already met by the pending signal infrastructure and state transitions (SIGSTOP->STOPPED, SIGCONT->RUNNING, terminating signals->ZOMBIE).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md shows VAL-PROCESS-020 through VAL-PROCESS-025 requirements; existing signal.c implementation already had state transitions, pending signal tracking, and parent notification - the pthread_kill addition is an enhancement for actual thread notification

summary: Feature 'process-table-signals' has been successfully implemented. The commit b89c67f04 adds pthread_kill() integration to deliver actual POSIX signals to target threads. The implementation correctly: (1) delivers signals via pthread_kill to target threads in __iox_apply_signal_to_task(), (2) maintains existing pending signal infrastructure, (3) properly handles state transitions (SIGSTOP->STOPPED, SIGCONT->RUNNING, terminating->ZOMBIE), (4) notifies parents via condition variables. All 206/211 tests pass (5 pre-existing failures in unrelated exec/file syscall areas). The validation contract assertions VAL-PROCESS-020 through VAL-PROCESS-025 are satisfied.
